zh.lifeSummary: 當Sancha De Castilla y Leon Queen of Aragón於1154年9月21日出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安利安時，她的父親Alfonso Raimundez VII Rey de Castilla y León49歲，母親Richeza of Poland25歲。她於1174年1月18日與Alphonse II roi d'Aragon在西班牙阿拉格昂札拉格薩結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1208年11月9日在西班牙阿拉格昂斯卡維拉紐瓦 德 希根亞享年54歲，埋葬在西班牙阿拉格昂斯卡斯卡。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sancha De Castilla y Leon Queen of Aragón于1154年9月21日出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安利安时，她的父亲Alfonso Raimundez VII Rey de Castilla y León49岁，母亲Richeza of Poland25岁。她于1174年1月18日与Alphonse II roi d'Aragon在西班牙阿拉格昂札拉格薩结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1208年11月9日在西班牙阿拉格昂斯卡維拉紐瓦 德 希根亞享年54岁，埋葬在西班牙阿拉格昂斯卡斯卡。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Castilla
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Alfredo, Carlos, Raul, Sergio, Jesus, Jorge, Pablo, Ruben, Blanca, Elido, Francisco, Guillermo.
Spanish: habitational name from Castile (Spanish Castilla), a region in central Spain. The name derives from the many castles in the region. This surname is also found in southern France.
History: As an independent kingdom between the 10th and 15th centuries, Castile formed the largest power in the Iberian peninsula.

variants.name: Castiglia
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Annamarie, Antonino, Benedetto, Carmine, Francesco, Gaetano, Gennaro, Giacomo, Giuseppe, Ignazio.
Italian: habitational name from Castile (Italian Castiglia) in central Spain (see Castilla ).

nameMeanings.name: Leon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Carlos, Pedro, Jesus, Jorge, Francisco, Rafael, Miguel, Raul.

Spanish (León): habitational name from León, a city in northwestern Spain, named with Latin legio, genitive legionis ‘legion’, a division of the Roman army. In Roman times the city was the garrison of the 7th Legion, known as the Legio Gemina. The city's name became shortened from Legion(em) to Leon(em), and in this form developed an unetymological association with the word for ‘lion’ (Spanish león).

French (Léon) and Spanish (León): from the personal name Léon, León, from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn. The name Leo(n) was borne by numerous early Christian martyrs and thirteen popes.

variants.name: Len
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Zbigniew, Jozef, Karol.
Polish and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a flax grower or merchant, from Polish len ‘flax’.
Polish (Leń): nickname from leń ‘lazybones’.

variants.name: Leonides
meanings: Hispanic (Leónides; Mexico): from the personal name Leónides, from Greek Leonidēs, itself most likely a patronymic derivative of Leōn (from leōn ‘lion’; see Leon ) + the suffix -(e)ides.

variants.name: Lyon
meanings: Scottish, English, and French: from the personal name Middle English Lyon, Old French Leon (from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn; see Leon ). Compare Lyall .
Scottish and English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Lyons-la-Forêt in Eure, Normandy. It is unlikely to be from the better-known southern French city of Lyon (see 5 below).

English and French: nickname from Middle English lioun ‘lion’ (Old English, Latin leo), Old French leon, perhaps applied to a brave, fierce, or proud person, or one with a shaggy mane of hair. Compare Lion .

variants.name: Leos
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Pedro, Francisco, Manuel, Ruben, Carlos, Juan, Santos, Jesus, Pablo, Raul, Alfonso.
Hispanic (mainly Mexico): altered form of Basque Leoz, itself a Castilianized form (also Léoz) of a habitational name from Leotz (in Castilian: Leoz) in Navarre.
Greek: from the personal name Leos, a pet form of Leon .

variants.name: Liang
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Jian, Feng, Wen, Wei, Ping, Ying, Cheng, Guang, Jin, Jing, Liang, Chang, Chung, Hu, Nam, Pang, Jang, Min, Neng, Shen, Shiu, Sieu, Yeong.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 梁, meaning ‘roofbeam’ or ‘bridge’ in Chinese: (i) from Liang (梁), the name of a state (located in present-day Hancheng in Shaanxi Province) which was granted to Kang, the youngest son of Qinzhong (died 822  BC ), because the latter made great contributions to the fight against minority ethnic groups in western China during the reign of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After the state was annihilated by the state of Qin in 641  BC , some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (ii) from the second element of the placenames Jie Liang (解梁), Gao Liang (高粱), and Qu Liang (曲梁), names of several cities originally within the border of the state of Jin (located mainly in present-day Shanxi province). In 645  BC , the Duke Hui of Jin (ruler of the state of Jin, died 637  BC ) ceded these cities to the state of Qin (located originally in present-day western Shaanxi province). Later, people from these cities adopted 粱 as their surname. (iii) from Liang (梁), also known as South Liang, the name of another state (located in present-day Longxi in Gansu Province) which was granted to Tang, son of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After this state was annihilated by the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (iii) from the Ba Lie Lan (拔列蘭) family, members of an ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surname to 梁 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

variants.name: Leonti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenic, Salvatore.
Italian (Sicily): from the Greek personal name or nickname Leon, Leontos ‘lion’ (equivalent of Latin Leo, Leonis; see Leon 2).

variants.name: Lew
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wing, Yuen, Fook, Jung, Ying, Young, Yung, Bin, Foon, Hong, Kam, Koon.
Polish: from the personal name Lew ‘lion’, adopted as a translation of Leon .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Lev  .

nameMeanings.name: Sancha
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: spelling of Sasha, Russian pet form of Alexander and Alexandra .

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: When Sancha De Castilla y Leon Queen of Aragón was born on 21 September 1154, in León, Castilla y León, Spain, her father, Alfonso Raimundez VII Rey de Castilla y León, was 49 and her mother, Richeza of Poland, was 25. She married Alphonse II roi d'Aragon on 18 January 1174, in Zaragoza, Aragón, Spain. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 9 November 1208, in Villanueva de Sigena, Huesca, Aragón, Spain, at the age of 54, and was buried in Huesca, Huesca, Aragón, Spain.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sancha De Castilla y Leon Queen of Aragón est née le 21 septembre 1154, à León, Castilla y León, Espagne, son père, Alfonso Raimundez VII Rey de Castilla y León, avait 49 ans et sa mère, Richeza of Poland, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Alphonse II roi d'Aragon le 18 janvier 1174, à Zaragoza, Aragón, Espagne. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 9 novembre 1208, à Villanueva de Sigena, Huesca, Aragón, Espagne, à l’âge de 54 ans, et a été enterrée à Huesca, Huesca, Aragón, Espagne.

de.Villanueva de Sigena, Huesca, Aragón, Es: Villanueva de Sigena, Huesca, Aragón, Spanien
de.lifeSummary: Als Sancha De Castilla y Leon Queen of Aragón am 21. September 1154 in León, Castilla y León, Spanien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Alfonso Raimundez VII Rey de Castilla y León 49 und ihre Mutter Richeza of Poland 25. Sie hat am 18. Januar 1174 in Zaragoza, Aragón, Spanien Alphonse II roi d'Aragon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 9. November 1208 im Alter von 54 Jahren in Villanueva de Sigena, Huesca, Aragón, Spanien und wurde in Huesca, Huesca, Aragón, Spanien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sancha De Castilla y Leon Queen of Aragón è nata il il 21 settembre 1154 nel León, Castilla y León, Spagna, sua padre, Alfonso Raimundez VII Rey de Castilla y León, aveva 49 anni e sua madre, Richeza of Poland, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato Alphonse II roi d'Aragon il 18 gennaio 1174, nel Zaragoza, Aragón, Spagna. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 9 novembre 1208, nel Villanueva de Sigena, Huesca, Aragón, Spagna, all'età di 54 anni, e fu sepolta a Huesca, Huesca, Aragón, Spagna.

ja.lifeSummary: Sancha De Castilla y Leon Queen of Aragónが1154年9月21日にスペインカスティラ イ レoンレoンで生まれた時，父Alfonso Raimundez VII Rey de Castilla y Leónは49歳，母Richeza of Polandは25歳であった。1174年1月18日にスペインアラグoンザラゴザでAlphonse II roi d'Aragonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1208年11月9日にスペインアラグoンハエスカヴィラヌエヴァ ド シジェナで54歳で死去し，スペインアラグoンハエスカハエスカにて埋葬された。

ko.Villanueva de Sigena, Huesca, Aragón, Es: 스페인 아라그o 후스카 비랜에바 데 시겐아
ko.lifeSummary: Sancha De Castilla y Leon Queen of Aragón(이)가 1154년 9월 21일 스페인 카스틸라 이 레o 레o에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Alfonso Raimundez VII Rey de Castilla y León(은)는 49세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Richeza of Poland(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1174년 1월 18일 스페인 아라그o 자라고자에서 Alphonse II roi d'Aragon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1208년 11월 9일 스페인 아라그o 후스카 비랜에바 데 시겐아에서 54세의 나이로 사망했으며 스페인 아라그o 후스카 후스카에 묻혔다.

pt.Villanueva de Sigena, Huesca, Aragón, Es: Villanueva de Sigena, Huesca, Aragón, Espanha
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sancha De Castilla y Leon Queen of Aragón nasceu em 21 de setembro de 1154, em León, Castilla y León, Espanha, seu pai, Alfonso Raimundez VII Rey de Castilla y León, tinha 49 anos e sua mãe, Richeza of Poland, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com Alphonse II roi d'Aragon em 18 de janeiro de 1174, em Zaragoza, Aragón, Espanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 9 de novembro de 1208, em Villanueva de Sigena, Huesca, Aragón, Espanha, com 54 anos, e foi sepultada em Huesca, Huesca, Aragón, Espanha.

ru.Villanueva de Sigena, Huesca, Aragón, Es: виллануева де сиджена, хуеска, арагoн, Испания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sancha De Castilla y Leon Queen of Aragón родилась 21 сентября 1154 г. в местности леoн, кастилла и леoн, Испания, её отцу по имени Alfonso Raimundez VII Rey de Castilla y León было 49 лет, а матери, которую звали Richeza of Poland, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Alphonse II roi d'Aragon 18 января 1174 г. в местности зарагоза, арагoн, Испания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла 9 ноября 1208 г. в местности виллануева де сиджена, хуеска, арагoн, Испания в возрасте 54 лет и была похоронена в местности хуеска, хуеска, арагoн, Испания.

es.Villanueva de Sigena, Huesca, Aragón, Es: Villanueva de Sigena, Huesca, Aragón, España
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sancha De Castilla y Leon Queen of Aragón nació el 21 de septiembre de 1154, en León, Castilla y León, España, su papá, Alfonso Raimundez VII Rey de Castilla y León, tenía 49 años y su mamá, Richeza of Poland, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Alphonse II roi d'Aragon el 18 de enero de 1174, en Zaragoza, Aragón, España. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 9 de noviembre de 1208, en Villanueva de Sigena, Huesca, Aragón, España, a la edad de 54 años, y fue sepultada en Huesca, Huesca, Aragón, España.
